preamble: Vocalization of [b] can result in the mid-back rounded vowel [o], particularly when the labial element merges with surrounding vowels or simplifies from an earlier [u] stage. This process is crosslinguistically rare and typically involves intermediate fricative or glide stages.

languageExamples.description: French historical development (e.g. Latin 'habere' > 'avoir' stages where labials vocalized to vowels/glides).

sources: Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology
